hypothesis: Augmenting the pharmacophore-guided reinforcement-learning reward with explicit 3D pharmacophore constraints derived from ensemble conformers of the reference estrogen receptor modulators will yield de novo molecules that, compared to the CATS-only method, exhibit significantly improved in vitro binding potency to ERα (median IC50 reduced by ≥2-fold, p<0.05) while preserving high structural novelty (≥80% of molecules with Tanimoto ≤0.4 to references) and comparable drug-likeness (mean QED within ±0.1).
